text.title: GitOps Delivery Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gitops methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Declarative manifests in Git are the single source of truth — cluster state is reconciled from Git, never edited live.
rule.rationale: Out-of-band kubectl edits create silent drift; reconciliation from Git makes the cluster diffable.

rule.statement: Split repos (or paths) into apps/ (Helm charts + Kustomize bases) and envs/ (per-environment values + overlays).
rule.rationale: Mixing app definition with env-specific values blocks promotion via simple PR; the split makes promotion a single-line change.

rule.statement: Configure the reconciler (Argo CD / Flux) with automated sync + self-heal + prune for non-prod and manual sync gating for prod.
rule.rationale: Auto-prune in prod is the documented foot-gun; manual gating keeps the human in the rollback loop.

rule.statement: Alert on detected drift between cluster and Git within 5 minutes and fail the pipeline if drift persists past the SLO.
rule.rationale: Silent drift is the failure mode that turns GitOps into theatre; the alert is the only honest detector.

rule.statement: Wire progressive rollout (Argo Rollouts / Flagger) for any service with measurable traffic, with metric-based analysis gating each step.
rule.rationale: Stepwise rollout with metric gating turns a bad deploy into a 1-2% blast radius instead of 100%.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
